intent: Propose one falsifiable comparative-statics question and an exact diagnostic benchmark set for consumable quality certificates, without solving or asserting any direction, validity, novelty, or welfare result.

entity.title: Can cheaper consumable certification generate non-monotone buyer outcomes?
entity.summary: A proposal to test whether certificate-stock depletion and the induced composition of the uncertified pool can overturn monotone comparative statics in certificate cost.

payload.phenomenon: The steady-state buyer inspection rate, successful-allocation probability, or ex ante buyer payoff net of inspection cost may have an interior reversal as the truthful-certificate cost k falls because issuance, certificate depletion after trade, and the high-capacity share among uncertified sellers jointly change the value of inspection.
payload.object_to_explain: Fix all non-k primitives and one k-invariant equilibrium-selection rule. For X(k) equal to the stationary probability of paid inspection S(k), the probability of successful service A(k), or buyer expected payoff U(k), determine whether there exist k1 < k2 < k3 for which [X(k2)-X(k1)] times [X(k3)-X(k2)] is strictly negative in the consumable-certificate game, and whether the reversal disappears in the channel-control benchmarks.
payload.unresolved_delta: It is unresolved whether any admissible common-primitives parameterization and selected stationary symmetric Markov-perfect equilibrium produces the strict sign reversal, which of S, A, or U reverses, and whether depletion-driven changes in the uncertified pool rather than cost accounting or a changing inspection policy are necessary for it.
payload.importance: Certificates are an endogenous, exhaustible information stock rather than a permanent label. Establishing the source of any reversal would distinguish the information-composition effect of certification from the direct saving in sellers' certification costs and from buyers' endogenous acquisition of information, which is necessary before interpreting a cheaper-certificate policy through search, matching, or buyer-payoff comparisons.
payload.kill_condition: Kill this proposed mechanism within the stated scope if a characterization of the selected stationary symmetric Markov-perfect equilibrium shows that each of S(k), A(k), and U(k) is weakly monotone on the admissible k interval for every fixed non-k primitive vector. Also reject a stock-depletion attribution for any computed reversal unless that reversal is absent when the certificate-stock and uncertified-composition law is frozen, and reject an endogenous-inspection attribution unless the full-model contrast differs from the fixed-inspection benchmark.
payload.proposed_scope: Two long-lived sellers and one short-lived buyer per period; seller capacity is H or L under one fixed public transition kernel, only H can buy a truthful one-unit certificate at cost k, an outstanding certificate publicly guarantees one successful service and is erased when its seller trades, an uncertified seller can be H or L, the buyer observes certificate states and may pay c to inspect one uncertified seller before buying from at most one seller or exiting, a successful service yields buyer value 1 and seller margin m, and sellers discount by delta. Compare k while holding c, m, delta, the capacity kernel, timing, public state, and a lexicographic stationary-strategy selection rule fixed. Treat all question and benchmark choices as L1 proposals for later human G1 review.
prohibited_claims: No direction of any comparative static is asserted.
No existence, uniqueness, proof, validation, or welfare conclusion is asserted.
No novelty or literature-positioning claim is asserted.
No reversal is attributed to certificate depletion without the declared paired controls.

entity.title: Channel controls for consumable certificate comparative statics
entity.summary: Four common-primitives games that isolate depletion of the public certificate stock, pure certification-cost accounting, and endogenous buyer inspection.

benchmarks.label: Full consumable stock with endogenous inspection
exact_primitives: Two long-lived sellers, one short-lived buyer per period, capacities H or L under the fixed kernel P, buyer success value 1, exit payoff 0, inspection cost c, successful-trade seller margin m, seller discount factor delta, and certificate cost k.

Only an H seller may issue a truthful public certificate; an outstanding certificate guarantees one successful service and its public stock indicator switches from one to zero immediately after trade with that seller.
The buyer observes both certificate indicators, may inspect at most one uncertified seller at cost c, then buys from at most one seller or exits.
All non-k primitives, the public state definition, and the equilibrium-selection rule are identical at every compared k.
timing: Public certificate stock is carried into the period and each seller observes its current private capacity.
Eligible H sellers choose whether to pay k and issue; the buyer observes certificate states.
The buyer chooses inspection, if any, observes the inspected uncertified capacity, then buys from one seller or exits.
Service and payoffs realize; trade with a certified seller consumes that certificate; the capacity and certificate state then transition.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov-perfect equilibrium, using one fixed lexicographic ordering of stationary strategy vectors for every k.
benchmarks.prediction: For each k, record S(k), A(k), U(k), the stationary certificate-stock distribution, and the stationary H share among uncertified sellers; test the exact adjacent-difference product for a strict sign reversal without presuming its sign.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether any of S, A, or U has a strict interior reversal and which state-composition movements accompany it.

benchmarks.label: Nondepleting-certificate control
exact_primitives: Use every primitive, information set, seller issuance choice, buyer action, payoff, capacity kernel, and equilibrium-selection rule from full_consumable_endogenous.
Change only the certificate transition after trade: a trade leaves the public certificate indicator unchanged for the certified capacity spell instead of consuming it.
Keep certification cost k and endogenous inspection available.
timing: Use the full-game timing through service and payoff realization.
At the state transition, do not erase a certificate merely because its seller traded; apply the common exogenous capacity-spell expiration rule thereafter.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov-perfect equilibrium under the same lexicographic selection rule.
benchmarks.prediction: Compare the sign pattern of each full-game finite difference with its nondepleting counterpart; this contrast turns off trade-induced certificate depletion while retaining endogenous issuance and inspection.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether removing only the trade-consumption transition eliminates, preserves, or changes any strict reversal.

benchmarks.label: Frozen stock and uncertified-composition cost control
exact_primitives: Choose and declare a baseline k0 and import from the full game at k0 the seller issuance rule and the stationary transition kernel over certificate indicators and the H share among uncertified sellers.
For every counterfactual k, hold that issuance rule and state-composition kernel exactly fixed; change k only as a debit in seller payoff accounting.
Let buyers best respond to the unchanged public-state and uncertified-composition law; keep all buyer payoffs and information costs fixed.
timing: Draw the public certificate and uncertified-composition state from the fixed k0 kernel.
Apply the fixed k0 issuance transition, let the buyer inspect or trade under the unchanged information environment, and debit the counterfactual k only to issuing sellers.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Stationary buyer best response to the frozen k0 seller and state law; no seller re-optimization as k varies.
benchmarks.prediction: Use this as a zero-buyer-environment accounting control: any k contrast in S, A, or U is tested against an information state and buyer payoff problem that are fixed by construction, while seller certification expenditure alone changes.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether a full-game reversal survives once all k-induced certificate-stock and uncertified-composition responses are removed.

benchmarks.label: Fixed-inspection-policy control
exact_primitives: Use the consumable certificate transition, endogenous seller issuance, capacity kernel, payoffs, and equilibrium-selection rule from full_consumable_endogenous.
Choose the same declared baseline k0 and freeze the buyer's state-contingent probability of paying c to inspect at its selected full-game k0 value for every counterfactual k.
After the frozen inspection draw and any signal, permit the buyer's purchase-versus-exit action to be a best response; sellers continue to optimize against the fixed inspection rule.
timing: Follow the full-game timing, but replace the buyer's inspection decision node by the fixed k0 state-contingent randomization.
Retain endogenous seller certification, purchase or exit after information, service, certificate consumption, and state transition.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Stationary seller Markov best responses and buyer purchase-or-exit best responses subject to the exogenously fixed k0 inspection policy, with the common selection rule.
benchmarks.prediction: Compare A(k), U(k), certificate stocks, and uncertified composition with the full game while S(k) is fixed by construction; the difference diagnoses the endogenous inspection-response margin without asserting a direction.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether any full-game reversal requires adjustment of the inspection policy rather than only seller issuance and certificate-stock composition.

payload.exact_question_delta: The full game asks for a strict sign reversal in S, A, or U. The nondepleting game changes only trade-induced certificate erasure; the frozen-stock game holds issuance and the uncertified-composition kernel fixed while k changes only seller cost accounting; the fixed-inspection game holds the inspection policy fixed while preserving consumable stocks and endogenous seller issuance. These three paired deltas separately diagnose depletion, mechanical cheaper certification, and endogenous inspection.

outcome.rationale: Proposes exactly one ResearchQuestion and one BenchmarkSet, links every benchmark delta back to that exact question version, and reserves all stable framing choices for later human G1 review.
